programmes.show_description: The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
Pansexual Sarah can't wait to road test her new body on her first date as a woman. Plumber Jamie's love life has gone down the u-bend, so he's all cisterns go to find a partner.
The chemistry's strong between naked cleaner Elleigha and perfectionist chef Louis. Sisters Caitlin and Lucy have never dated before. And can joker Asher heal his past wounds?
Experts sift through crammed homes looking for lost treasure worth life-changing sums. At a 700-year-old Scottish castle, there's an astonishing discovery featuring a rare painting. (Ep1/3)
...Less: Joe and Suzanne help single working mum of six Vicky to regain control in the kitchen. On the menu: a smoky bean cobbler, chicken parmigiana and more.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
It's the first night in Kent and clairvoyant Yvette hosts. What do her guests think of her machine for bustin' ghosts? #WorldPoetryDay
Two of John's guests have a dating-etiquette feud. But what do they think of his psychedelic food? #WorldPoetryDay
Are Yemi's guests ready to savour, her Nigerian spice and her Nigerian flavour? #WorldPoetryDay
There was once was a host in north Kent, who was feeling a bit confident. He may not be stressed, but with guests unimpressed, it looks like his chances are spent. #WorldPoetryDay
Lisa goes French, can her guests believe their eyes, when they see her human roulette and eat her coq surprise? #WorldPoetryDay
She says she's a drunk Martha Stewart but her name's really Michelle. Will our host's Portuguese-American-Italian food go down well? #WorldPoetryDay
Is adventurous Dave's cheesecake cocktail, culinary genius or epic food fail? #WorldPoetryDay
Martina has a guest, no fan of fruit or curry. Martina's serving both; do you think she should worry? #WorldPoetryDay
Shane's food is class, Shane's martinis are strong. It's a shame Shane's guests just can't get along. #WorldPoetryDay
Is Michaela's Italian better than Martina's curry? Will it give confident Shane reason to worry? #WorldPoetryDay
A grand hotel in Dunoon has stunning views of the Firth. But do costly surprises make the makeover more than it's worth? #WorldPoetryDay
Bernie and Vernon have been together a while and are looking for a place on a Canary isle. Both are therapists keen to seal a deal. In the end, how does it make them feel? #WorldPoetryDay
Lanzarote lovers Trish and Ruth, are ready to settle under a Spanish roof. Surely there's no harm in, buying near Puerto del Carmen? Sara Damergi offers the proof. #WorldPoetryDay

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: There once were two pairs of buyers, with a list of holiday home desires. Did their ambitions come to fruition? Our what-happened-next team enquires. #WorldPoetryDay

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Do Alison and Brian in sunny Dénia, wish they'd opted for somewhere spennier? And a duo in Cyprus reveal, whether they like their home's penthouse feel. #WorldPoetryDay

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: After realising village life isn't for them, Sue and Tony bagged an inner-city gem. A year on, success or bungle? And hold on a second - is that bedroom a jungle?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: A family of six with one bathroom, think of the queues. But they've got lovely neighbours they don't want to lose... #WorldPoetryDay
Emma finally has the chance to escape. Take Mac's offer and make a clean break. But can she bear to leave Edwin behind? Emma Harte's not the giving-up kind... (Ep3) #WorldPoetryDay
Re-inventing herself in a bustling town, nothing can keep our Emma Harte down. But her biggest challenge yet is still to come. Can she have it all as a single mum? (Ep4) #WorldPoetryDay
Kaeden's feverish. Will Paul lose his thumb today? In the hospital. #WorldPoetryDay

On the 'Box this time: The Assembly, Genius Game, The Piano, Scam Interceptors, The Americas, Married at First Sight Australia and BBC News (noisy phones).
(2021) Feelgood coming-of-age musical with Max Harwood, Sarah Lancashire and Richard E Grant. A young lad dreams of escaping his humdrum existence and becoming a fabulous drag queen.
Jamie Oliver makes a one-pan salmon pie.
Jamie Oliver makes a gnarly chicken pie.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Best friends Richard and Susan host at the Blackthorn Farm B&B on Anglesey, where some guests feel the vibes for their morning meditation, but others feel the squeeze in their bathroom.
Graham and Barbara pride themselves on offering basic facilities for great value at the White Swan Hotel in West Yorkshire. But will their guests be happy to pay a supplement for breakfast?
Malcolm and Fiona hope to show their guests an alternative way of life at The Old Mill House in Bridport, Dorset. But some aren't convinced and the meditation session gets a mixed response.
Rachel and Dan Lonergan host at The Brighton Inn, where the guests clown around as they learn some circus skills. But some of them are on the offensive...
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to confront their rivals. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Louise and Daga offer breathtaking views at their place in Cornwall. But forget what's out the window - the B&Bers are preoccupied with their tellies.
Visit two is to twins Sara and Paula's hotel in Devon. After an afternoon on the lash at a local vineyard, are there sour grapes in the feedback?
Delightful décor, stunning views, sumptuous sausages and proper milk await the B&Bers at Simon and Vanessa's coastal guesthouse in Weymouth.
The week's final visit is to Jordan and Robin's country pub in Somerset. But with one B&Ber dreading the potential of stale ale smell, the hosts have their work cut out.
It's payment day: a chance for the B&Bers to complain about specks of dust that they've somehow found after trawling their rooms with a high-powered magnifying glass.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Hamilton, Scott and Jaqui's once-perfect pad is now a domestic battleground. With compromise in short supply, can Kirstie nail it with the extra space they need?
In north London, Kirstie Allsopp helps Digi to find a future-proof home, while Phil Spencer's with Rebecca and Sohail, whose rental is worth £200k more than their budget to buy.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Danni Menzies returns to Dénia to check in on Alison, Brian and their dogs. Jasmine Harman heads back to sun-kissed Kapparis for a catch-up with Bill and Sandra.
Gill and Chris are celebrating their 13th wedding anniversary in style by finding their very own cave house in Granada's gorgeous countryside. Can Laura Hamilton deliver the perfect place?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Ben Hillman learns how Nicola and Paul turned their Lanzarote 'happy place' into a sleek home. And Leah Charles-King is on the Costa del Sol with Kim and Debbie.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jean Johansson is back in Portugal with Alison and Mike, who she helped find their dream home. And Ben Hillman catches up with Whithney and Rose in Punta Prima.
It's double trouble for the Bodleys, as cockapoo twins Milo and Mabel burst into the pen. Can canine and family energy levels match? And Floyd the stray lurcher needs a good feed.

(2015) Part five of the Tom Cruise action-thriller franchise. The secret agents must bring down a terrorist group, but that's not easy when the CIA are hunting you down...
